title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dermot Crawford questions Marina Gregg regarding her distracted behavior during a reception, specifically her failure to respond to Mrs. Baskerville. Marina attributes her behavior to a "cold scene," a psychological blankness she experiences due to fatigue and the mechanical nature of her acting profession. Crawford expresses concern for Marina's safety, noting the threatening letters and the attempted poisoning, and suggests the perpetrator may be someone close to the house who suffers from a mental disorder. Marina's husband, Jason Rad, urges her to disclose any suspicions she may have.

Detective Chief Inspector Craddock questions Marina about her reaction to the arrival of Adwick Fenn and Laura Bruster. Marina admits she was happy to see them. Craddock reveals that Laura Bruster is the ex-wife of Marina's third husband, Robert Truscott. Marina acknowledges that Laura once made a drunken threat involving a gun but insists the conflict is ancient history. Jason Rad defends Laura, stating he was standing close enough to Marina to ensure she had no opportunity to poison her drink. Regarding Adwick Fenn, Jason describes him as a prominent figure in the American entertainment industry and an old friend.

Elsewhere, while weeding her garden, Mrs. Bantry observes Ella Jelinecki emerging from a phone booth. When Mrs. Bantry greets her, Ella appears startled and explains she was using the phone because of a problem with her home line. After Ella departs, a suspicious Mrs. Bantry decides to satisfy her curiosity by using the phone to call an American number.
